task_completion.visual_evidence: The UV Editor workspace is visible, showing the flattened, unwrapped 2D faces of the crate object.

task_completion.description: Create a 1024x1024 black image texture named 'wear_mask'
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Image Editor or an Image Texture node in the Shader Editor shows a newly created image named 'wear_mask' with a solid black background.

task_completion.description: Paint white wear details along the edges and corners of the crate
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Texture Paint workspace or Image Editor shows white brush strokes painted on the black 'wear_mask' image, specifically targeting the areas corresponding to the edges and corners of the crate.

task_completion.description: Configure the material to mix blue paint and grey metal using the wear mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Shader Editor shows a Mix node (Mix Color or Mix Shader) combining a blue base color and a grey metallic color. The 'wear_mask' Image Texture node is connected to the 'Fac' (Factor) input of this Mix node.
The 'Save Image As' dialog in Blender shows the file being saved as 'wear_mask.png' in the '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' directory, or a terminal/file explorer confirms the file's creation.
The Blender window title bar displays 'distressed_crate.blend', or the 'Save As' dialog shows the file being saved to the expected '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/' path.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender GUI to complete the task
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active use of Blender's Texture Paint workspace, Shader Editor, and UV Editor, rather than relying solely on Python scripts in the text editor or terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent manually painted the wear mask
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the brush tool selected and active painting progress in the 3D viewport or Image Editor, rather than importing a pre-existing image file.

integrity.description: The agent used Blender's internal save functions
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show Blender's 'Image > Save As' and 'File > Save As' menus or dialogs being used, rather than terminal commands renaming or moving unrelated files to match expected outputs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Object name: 'SciFiCrate'. Expected image save path: '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/wear_mask.png'. Expected project save path: '/home/ga/BlenderProjects/distressed_crate.blend'. The material must use the 'wear_mask' image as a mix factor between a blue color and a grey metallic color.
